summary: Security review of CC-0116 (per-ControlPlane service DB-credential scoping). My focus area (tenant isolation/blast radius, secrets handling, RBAC, OpenBao policies/least-privilege, TLS, injection) is CLEAN. Per-ControlPlane isolation is correctly implemented and proven: dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor embeds BOTH cp.Namespace and cp.Name ('openstack/keystone/%s/%s/db'), exactly mirroring the admin precedent adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor ('openstack/keystone/%s/%s/admin/app-credential'); dbCredentialSecretName derives from keystoneName(cp) (cp.Name + '-keystone-db-credentials'). The data-driven TestDBCredentialRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane proves no two ControlPlanes collide on either the OpenBao path or the materialised Secret, so the old shared blast radius of the deleted flat 'openstack/keystone/db' ExternalSecret is eliminated. No secret values are logged or hardcoded: the two G101 nolint annotations (dbCredentialSecretNameSuffix='-db-credentials', conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady='DBCredentialsReady') are legitimate false positives. The bootstrap DB password is generated in-pod via the @generate marker (sys/tools/random), never passed as a kubectl-exec CLI arg, and the kv_path is injected via env var to avoid shell injection. Both changed OpenBao policies (eso-management.hcl, eso-control-plane.hcl) remain READ-ONLY with only comment additions and no path widening; eso-management correctly scopes to keystone/* (least privilege) rather than openstack/*. The ExternalSecret is owner-referenced (SetControllerReference in the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure), CreationPolicy Owner, ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store. The extra mark_eso_managed on the read-only DB path is inert and not a security problem: no push policy grants write to the 'db' leaf (push-keystone-keys.hcl globs require a literal /fernet-keys or /credential-keys suffix), so no ESO/PushSecret token can ever overwrite the DB credential. APPROVED for the security slice.

tests_checklist.item: Per-ControlPlane isolation unit test exists and asserts distinct keys across namespaces and distinct secret names across names (TestDBCredentialRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane)
Brownfield path test asserts NO ExternalSecret projected and DBCredentialsReady=True (no OpenBao reference)
Managed-mode test asserts owner-reference, CreationPolicy Owner, ClusterSecretStore ref, and per-CP remoteRef key for both username and password
e2e catch block dumps Secret with describe (keys only) and never echoes decoded secret values
e2e/missing-secret fixture updated from flat shared path to per-CP scoped path (no leftover shared-key consumer)

code_quality_checklist.item: dbCredentialExternalSecret is a pure builder; owner-ref set in reconciler mutate closure (separation of concerns)
Condition messages/logs carry only metadata (names, paths, %v of K8s API errors), no secret payloads

security_checklist.item: Tenant isolation: remote key embeds cp.Namespace AND cp.Name; no cross-CP collision (proven by distinctness test)
Secret name derived from keystoneName(cp) so two CPs in one namespace never share a materialised Secret
Matches admin-credential precedent (adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor) for scoping discipline
No hardcoded secrets; G101 nolints on suffix/condition-type are legitimate false positives
No secret values logged anywhere in reconcile_dbcredentials.go or WaitForExternalSecret
Bootstrap DB password generated in-pod via @generate (sys/tools/random), not a kubectl-exec CLI arg
kv_path injected via BAO_KV_PATH env var to prevent shell injection into sh -c
db_path seed mirrors admin_path seed (symmetric, idempotent write_secret_if_missing)
Extra mark_eso_managed on read-only DB path is inert: no push policy grants write to the db leaf; no exploitable capability
OpenBao eso-management.hcl read grants unchanged; trailing-* wildcard already covers per-CP path; no widening
eso-management scoped to keystone/* not openstack/* (least privilege; nova/neutron etc excluded)
eso-control-plane.hcl openstack/* read grant already covers per-CP db path; read-only; no widening
ExternalSecret owner-referenced via SetControllerReference (GC wired) and CreationPolicy Owner
ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store used (TLS-backed cluster store, VAULT_CACERT in bootstrap)
RBAC not widened: helm only adds test assertions over the existing externalsecrets create/update rule reused from the admin AC ExternalSecret
cp.Spec untouched; per-CP secretRef override applied only to projected Keystone child under managed-mode guard (ClusterRef != nil)
Deleted keystone-db.yaml removed the shared flat-path ExternalSecret; kustomization entry removed (no dangling shared blast radius)

architecture_checklist.item: DBCredentials sub-reconciler ordered after Infrastructure and before Keystone so the scoped Secret materialises before Keystone consumes its secretRef

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Reuses openBaoClusterStoreName, childNamespace, keystoneName, WaitForExternalSecret rather than reinventing
Mirrors admin-credential naming/scoping discipline instead of a divergent scheme

fail_fast_checklist.item: KORC_CONTROLPLANES identity validated to be '<namespace>/<controlplane>' form, exits 1 otherwise
ExternalSecret error and not-ready paths set DBCredentialsReady=False with distinct reasons and requeue/return error

defensive_checklist.item: Non-generated bootstrap values single-quote-escaped before sh -c interpolation
WaitForExternalSecret inspects only conditions, never .Data; missing ES returns (false,nil) not a panic

suggested_improvements: Optional: the inert mark_eso_managed on db_path (write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:184) could be dropped until issue #439 wires a DB-rotation PushSecret, removing a no-op step and the 'Reviewer: please verify' marker. Not a security defect — it confers no write capability because no push policy grants write to the db leaf — but removing dead-until-#439 calls keeps the seed minimal. The accompanying DECISION comment already documents the rationale, so keeping it for symmetry is also acceptable.

Out of strict diff scope but worth a future tidy: push-keystone-keys.hcl comment (lines 33-48) still describes the read-only DB credential path as the older single-segment 'kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/{namespace}/db'; CC-0116's actual DB path is namespace+name-scoped ('.../{namespace}/{name}/db'). The write globs require a literal /fernet-keys or /credential-keys suffix so neither shape can match — no functional or security impact — but the stale prose could mislead a future reader auditing blast radius.
next_steps: No security blockers. Proceed; the per-CP isolation, least-privilege OpenBao policies, owner-ref/CreationPolicy Owner, in-pod password generation, and injection-safe bootstrap are all verified.
detected_patterns: per-tenant secret-path scoping by Namespace+Name (mirrors adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor)
ExternalSecret CreationPolicy Owner + controller owner-reference for GC
in-pod credential generation via @generate marker (no cleartext in process args)
env-var injection of untrusted path into sh -c (shell-injection avoidance)
read-only OpenBao policy with trailing-* wildcard pre-covering deeper per-CP paths (no widening)
least-privilege scoping keystone/* over openstack/* in eso-management
G101 nolint on name-suffix/condition-type string constants (documented false positives)
